perturbation.original_text: a.As of the VNBJ Closing, except as expressly set forth in this Amendment, the JV Agreement shall immediately cease to have any application or effect with respect to VNBJ, all rights and obligations with respect to VNBJ under the JV Agreement shall terminate and all references to "Company" or "Companies" in the JV Agreement (either in the body of the JV Agreement or its schedules and other attachments) shall read and be interpreted to mean VNBZ and/or ANRA except as the context otherwise requires.
perturbation.changed_text: a.As of the VNBJ Closing, the JV Agreement shall immediately cease to have any application or effect with respect to VNBJ, all rights and obligations with respect to VNBJ under the JV Agreement shall terminate. However, references to "Company" or "Companies" in the JV Agreement shall continue to include VNBJ for the purposes of ongoing audits and potential future claims related to activities prior to the VNBJ Closing, as the context requires.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text states that the JV Agreement ceases to have any effect with respect to VNBJ after the closing. However, the modified text states that references to 'Company' or 'Companies' will continue to include VNBJ for audits and potential claims, effectively keeping parts of the JV Agreement active for VNBJ. This creates uncertainty in enforcement because it is not clear which specific parts of the JV Agreement remain applicable and how they will be interpreted.
perturbation.location: Article 1.Amendment and Termination of JV Agreement
perturbation.contradicted_location: Article 1.Amendment and Termination of JV Agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 1.Amendment and Termination of JV Agreement a.As of the VNBJ Closing, the JV Agreement shall immediately cease to have any application or effect with respect to VNBJ, all rights and obligations with respect to VNBJ under the JV Agreement shall terminate. However, references to "Company" or "Companies" in the JV Agreement shall continue to include VNBJ for the purposes of ongoing audits and potential future claims related to activities prior to the VNBJ Closing, as the context requires. b.As of the VNBZ Closing, except as expressly set forth in this Amendment, the JV Agreement shall immediately cease to have any application or effect with respect to VNBZ, all rights and obligations with respect to VNBZ under the JV Agreement shall terminate and all references to "Company" or "Companies" in the JV Agreement (either in the body of the JV Agreement or its schedules and other attachments) shall read and be interpreted to mean VNBJ and/or ANRA except as the context otherwise requires. c.Except as expressly set forth in this Amendment and notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in the JV Agreement (including Section 9.3.11 (Termination and Survival)), (a) effective as of the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the JV Agreement shall terminate in its entirety with respect to the Veoneer Parties and (b) after the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the Veoneer Parties shall cease to be a party to the JV Agreement and shall have no further obligations with respect thereto. For clarity, upon the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the JV Agreement will remain in full force and effect in accordance with the terms thereof solely between the Nissin Parties and Autoliv AB with respect to the liquidation proceedings of ANRA.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. Both sections are identical in stating that the JV Agreement immediately ceases to have any application or effect with respect to VNBJ. However, both sections also state that references to "Company" or "Companies" will continue to include VNBJ for audits and potential claims, which keeps parts of the JV Agreement active for VNBJ. This introduces an in-text contradiction, as the JV Agreement is simultaneously terminated and partially active, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: d.Notwithstanding Article 1.1, Article 1.2 and Article 1.3 above, the termination of the JV Agreement with respect to VNBJ, VNBZ or the Veoneer Parties shall not release any Veoneer Party or any Nissin Party from liability for the breach of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or agreements set forth in the JV Agreement that arise prior to the VNBJ Closing or the VNBZ Closing, as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: d.Notwithstanding Article 1.1, Article 1.2 and Article 1.3 above, the termination of the JV Agreement with respect to VNBJ, VNBZ or the Veoneer Parties shall only release the Veoneer Party and the Nissin Party from liability for breach of any representation, warranties, covenants or agreements set forth in the JV Agreement that arise prior to the VNBJ Closing or the VNBZ Closing if such breach is disclosed to the other party in writing at least 30 days prior to the respective closing.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a condition (disclosure of breaches 30 days prior to closing) for the release of liability that wasn't present in the original clause. The original text states a general rule, while the modified text creates an exception that contradicts the scope of the original clause, now requiring advance disclosure for the liability release to be valid. The effect is uncertainty about the enforceability of claims arising from undisclosed breaches.
perturbation.location: Article 1.Amendment and Termination of JV Agreement
perturbation.contradicted_location: Article 1.Amendment and Termination of JV Agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 1.Amendment and Termination of JV Agreement c.Except as expressly set forth in this Amendment and notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in the JV Agreement (including Section 9.3.11 (Termination and Survival)), (a) effective as of the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the JV Agreement shall terminate in its entirety with respect to the Veoneer Parties and (b) after the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the Veoneer Parties shall cease to be a party to the JV Agreement and shall have no further obligations with respect thereto. For clarity, upon the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the JV Agreement will remain in full force and effect in accordance with the terms thereof solely between the Nissin Parties and Autoliv AB with respect to the liquidation proceedings of ANRA. d.Notwithstanding Article 1.1, Article 1.2 and Article 1.3 above, the termination of the JV Agreement with respect to VNBJ, VNBZ or the Veoneer Parties shall only release the Veoneer Party and the Nissin Party from liability for breach of any representation, warranties, covenants or agreements set forth in the JV Agreement that arise prior to the VNBJ Closing or the VNBZ Closing if such breach is disclosed to the other party in writing at least 30 days prior to the respective closing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a new condition for the release of liability (disclosure of breaches 30 days prior to closing) that was not present in the original agreement. This directly contradicts the original agreement, specifically as it relates to the section provided as 'Contradicted Text', which now has conflicting implications for liability release. The original text, and the contradicted text, imply a clear-cut termination of obligations, while the change introduces a scenario where obligations might persist if undisclosed breaches exist. This meets the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections now provide conflicting requirements for the release of liability.
